sections.label: Section 750
sections.original: εὔκαρπον ἐκβάλλουσι Θηβαίων στάχυν ·
sections.translation: produce the bountiful Theban crop . And falling like soldiers upon Hysiae and Erythrae , towns situated below the rock of Kithairon , they turned everything upside down . They were snatching children from their homes ;

sections.label: Section 755
sections.original: ὁπόσα δʼ ἐπʼ ὤμοις ἔθεσαν , οὐ δεσμῶν ὕπο
sections.translation: and whatever they put on their shoulders , whether bronze or iron , was not held on by bonds , nor did it fall to the ground . They carried fire on their locks , but it did not burn them . Some people in rage took up arms , being plundered by the Bacchae ,

sections.label: Section 760
sections.original: οὗπερ τὸ δεινὸν ἦν θέαμʼ ἰδεῖν , ἄναξ .
sections.translation: and the sight of this was terrible to behold , lord . For their pointed spears drew no blood , but the women , hurling the thyrsoi from their hands , kept wounding them and turned them to flight — women did this to men , not without the help of some god .

sections.label: Section 765
sections.original: πάλιν δʼ ἐχώρουν ὅθεν ἐκίνησαν πόδα ,
sections.translation: And they returned where they had come from , to the very fountains which the god had sent forth for them , and washed off the blood , and snakes cleaned the drops from the women ’ s cheeks with their tongues . Receive this god then , whoever he is ,

sections.label: Section 770
sections.original: δέχου πόλει τῇδʼ · ὡς τά τʼ ἄλλʼ ἐστὶν μέγας ,
sections.translation: into this city , master . For he is great in other respects , and they say this too of him , as I hear , that he gives to mortals the vine that puts an end to grief . Without wine there is no longer Aphrodite or any other pleasant thing for men .

sections.label: Section 775
sections.original: ταρβῶ μὲν εἰπεῖν τοὺς λόγους ἐλευθέρους
sections.translation: I fear to speak freely to the king , but I will speak nevertheless : Dionysus is inferior to none of the gods .

sections.label: Section 778
sections.original: ἤδη τόδʼ ἐγγὺς ὥστε πῦρ ὑφάπτεται
sections.translation: Already like fire does this insolence of the Bacchae blaze up , a great reproach for the Hellenes .

sections.label: Section 780
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐκ ὀκνεῖν δεῖ · στεῖχʼ ἐπʼ Ἠλέκτρας ἰὼν
sections.translation: But we must not hesitate . Go to the Electran gates , bid all the shield-bearers and riders of swift-footed horses to assemble , as well as all who brandish the light shield and pluck bowstrings with their hands , so that we can make an assault against

sections.label: Section 785
sections.original: βάκχαισιν · οὐ γὰρ ἀλλʼ ὑπερβάλλει τάδε ,
sections.translation: the Bacchae . For it is indeed too much if we suffer what we are suffering at the hands of women .

sections.label: Section 787
sections.original: πείθῃ μὲν οὐδέν , τῶν ἐμῶν λόγων κλύων ,
sections.translation: Pentheus , though you hear my words , you obey not at all . Though I suffer ill at your hands , still I say that it is not right for you to raise arms against a god ,

sections.label: Section 790
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἡσυχάζειν · Βρόμιος οὐκ ἀνέξεται
sections.translation: but to remain calm . Bromius will not allow you to remove the Bacchae from the joyful mountains .

sections.label: Section 792
sections.original: οὐ μὴ φρενώσεις μʼ , ἀλλὰ δέσμιος φυγὼν
sections.translation: Do not instruct me , but be content in your escape from prison . Or shall I bring punishment upon you again ?

sections.label: Section 794
sections.original: θύοιμʼ ἂν αὐτῷ μᾶλλον ἢ θυμούμενος
sections.translation: I would sacrifice to the god rather

sections.label: Section 795
sections.original: πρὸς κέντρα λακτίζοιμι θνητὸς ὢν θεῷ .
sections.translation: than kick against his spurs in anger , a mortal against a god .

sections.label: Section 796
sections.original: θύσω , φόνον γε θῆλυν , ὥσπερ ἄξιαι ,
sections.translation: I will sacrifice , making a great slaughter of the women , as they deserve , in the glens of Kithairon .

sections.label: Section 798
sections.original: φεύξεσθε πάντες · καὶ τόδʼ αἰσχρόν , ἀσπίδας
sections.translation: You will all flee . And it will be a source of shame that you turn your bronze shields away from the thyrsoi of the Bacchae .

sections.label: Section 800
sections.original: ἀπόρῳ γε τῷδε συμπεπλέγμεθα ξένῳ ,
sections.translation: This stranger with whom I am locked together is impossible , and neither suffering nor doing will he be quiet .

sections.label: Section 802
sections.original: ὦ τᾶν , ἔτʼ ἔστιν εὖ καταστῆσαι τάδε .
sections.translation: My friend , there is still opportunity to arrange these things well .

sections.label: Section 803
sections.original: τί δρῶντα ; δουλεύοντα δουλείαις ἐμαῖς ;
sections.translation: Doing what ? Being a slave to my slaves ?

sections.label: Section 804
sections.original: ἐγὼ γυναῖκας δεῦρʼ ὅπλων ἄξω δίχα .
sections.translation: Without weapons I will bring the women here .

sections.label: Section 805
sections.original: οἴμοι · τόδʼ ἤδη δόλιον ἔς με μηχανᾷ .
sections.translation: Alas ! You are contriving this as a trick against me .

sections.label: Section 806
sections.original: ποῖόν τι , σῷσαί σʼ εἰ θέλω τέχναις ἐμαῖς ;
sections.translation: What sort , if I wish to save you by my contrivances ?

sections.label: Section 807
sections.original: ξυνέθεσθε κοινῇ τάδʼ , ἵνα βακχεύητʼ ἀεί .
sections.translation: You have devised this together , so that you may have your revelry forever .

sections.label: Section 808
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ξυνεθέμην — τοῦτό γʼ ἔστι — τῷ θεῷ .
sections.translation: I certainly did — that is so — with the god .

sections.label: Section 809
sections.original: ἐκφέρετέ μοι δεῦρʼ ὅπλα , σὺ δὲ παῦσαι λέγων .
sections.translation: To a servant Bring me my armor . To Dionysus And you , stop speaking .

sections.label: Section 810
sections.original: ἆ .
sections.translation: Ah ! Do you wish to see them sitting together in the mountains ?

sections.label: Section 812
sections.original: μάλιστα , μυρίον γε δοὺς χρυσοῦ σταθμόν .
sections.translation: Certainly . I ’ d give an enormous amount of gold for that .

sections.label: Section 813
sections.original: τί δʼ εἰς ἔρωτα τοῦδε πέπτωκας μέγαν ;
sections.translation: Why do you desire this so badly ?

sections.label: Section 814
sections.original: λυπρῶς νιν εἰσίδοιμʼ ἂν ἐξῳνωμένας .
sections.translation: I would be sorry to see them in their drunkenness .

sections.label: Section 815
sections.original: ὅμως δʼ ἴδοις ἂν ἡδέως ἅ σοι πικρά ;
sections.translation: But would you see gladly what is grievous to you ?

sections.label: Section 816
sections.original: σάφʼ ἴσθι , σιγῇ γʼ ὑπʼ ἐλάταις καθήμενος .
sections.translation: To be sure , sitting quietly under the pines .

sections.label: Section 817
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐξιχνεύσουσίν σε , κἂν ἔλθῃς λάθρᾳ .
sections.translation: But they will track you down , even if you go in secret .

sections.label: Section 818
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐμφανῶς · καλῶς γὰρ ἐξεῖπας τάδε .
sections.translation: You are right : I will go openly .

sections.label: Section 819
sections.original: ἄγωμεν οὖν σε κἀπιχειρήσεις ὁδῷ ;
sections.translation: Shall I guide you ? Will you attempt the journey ?

sections.label: Section 820
sections.original: ἄγʼ ὡς τάχιστα , τοῦ χρόνου δέ σοι φθονῶ .
sections.translation: Lead me as quickly as possible . I grudge you the time .

sections.label: Section 821
sections.original: στεῖλαί νυν ἀμφὶ χρωτὶ βυσσίνους πέπλους .
sections.translation: Put linen clothes on your body then .

sections.label: Section 822
sections.original: τί δὴ τόδʼ ; ἐς γυναῖκας ἐξ ἀνδρὸς τελῶ ;
sections.translation: What is this ? Shall I then , instead of a man , be reckoned among the women ?

sections.label: Section 823
sections.original: μή σε κτάνωσιν , ἢν ἀνὴρ ὀφθῇς ἐκεῖ .
sections.translation: Lest they kill you if you are seen there as a man .

sections.label: Section 824
sections.original: εὖ γʼ εἶπας αὖ τόδʼ · ὥς τις εἶ πάλαι σοφός .
sections.translation: Again you speak correctly : how wise you have been all along !

sections.label: Section 825
sections.original: Διόνυσος ἡμᾶς ἐξεμούσωσεν τάδε .
sections.translation: Dionysus taught me these things fully .

sections.label: Section 826
sections.original: πῶς οὖν γένοιτʼ ἂν ἃ σύ με νουθετεῖς καλῶς ;
sections.translation: How can your advice to me be well carried out ?

sections.label: Section 827
sections.original: ἐγὼ στελῶ σε δωμάτων ἔσω μολών .
sections.translation: I will go inside and dress you .

sections.label: Section 828
sections.original: τίνα στολήν ; ἦ θῆλυν ; ἀλλʼ αἰδώς μʼ ἔχει .
sections.translation: In what clothing ? Female ? But shame holds me back .

sections.label: Section 829
sections.original: οὐκέτι θεατὴς μαινάδων πρόθυμος εἶ .
sections.translation: Are you no longer eager to view the maenads ?

sections.label: Section 830
sections.original: στολὴν δὲ τίνα φῂς ἀμφὶ χρῶτʼ ἐμὸν βαλεῖν ;
sections.translation: What clothing do you bid me to put on my body ?

sections.label: Section 831
sections.original: κόμην μὲν ἐπὶ σῷ κρατὶ ταναὸν ἐκτενῶ .
sections.translation: I will spread out hair at length on your head .

sections.label: Section 832
sections.original: τὸ δεύτερον δὲ σχῆμα τοῦ κόσμου τί μοι ;
sections.translation: What is the second part of my outfit ?

sections.label: Section 833
sections.original: πέπλοι ποδήρεις · ἐπὶ κάρᾳ δʼ ἔσται μίτρα .
sections.translation: A robe down to your feet . And you will wear a headband .

sections.label: Section 834
sections.original: ἦ καί τι πρὸς τοῖσδʼ ἄλλο προσθήσεις ἐμοί ;
sections.translation: And what else will you add to this for me ?

sections.label: Section 835
sections.original: θύρσον γε χειρὶ καὶ νεβροῦ στικτὸν δέρας .
sections.translation: A thyrsos in your hand , and a dappled fawn-skin .

sections.label: Section 836
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν δυναίμην θῆλυν ἐνδῦναι στολήν .
sections.translation: I could not put on a woman ’ s dress .

sections.label: Section 837
sections.original: ἀλλʼ αἷμα θήσεις συμβαλὼν βάκχαις μάχην .
sections.translation: But you will shed blood if you join battle with the Bacchae .

sections.label: Section 838
sections.original: ὀρθῶς · μολεῖν χρὴ πρῶτον εἰς κατασκοπήν .
sections.translation: True . We must go first and spy .

sections.label: Section 839
sections.original: σοφώτερον γοῦν ἢ κακοῖς θηρᾶν κακά .
sections.translation: This is at any rate wiser than hunting trouble with trouble .

sections.label: Section 840
sections.original: καὶ πῶς διʼ ἄστεως εἶμι Καδμείους λαθών ;
sections.translation: And how will I go through the city without being seen by the Thebans ?

sections.label: Section 841
sections.original: ὁδοὺς ἐρήμους ἴμεν · ἐγὼ δʼ ἡγήσομαι .
sections.translation: We will go on deserted roads . I will lead you .

sections.label: Section 842
sections.original: πᾶν κρεῖσσον ὥστε μὴ ʼγγελᾶν βάκχας ἐμοί .
sections.translation: Anything is better than to be mocked by the Bacchae . We two will go into the house . . . and I will consider what seems best .

sections.label: Section 844
sections.original: ἔξεστι · πάντῃ τό γʼ ἐμὸν εὐτρεπὲς πάρα .
sections.translation: It will be so ; in any case I am ready .

sections.label: Section 845
sections.original: στείχοιμʼ ἄν · ἢ γὰρ ὅπλʼ ἔχων πορεύσομαι
sections.translation: I will go in . For either I will go bearing arms , or I will obey your counsels .

sections.label: Section 848
sections.original: γυναῖκες , ἁνὴρ ἐς βόλον καθίσταται ,
sections.translation: Women , the man is caught in our net . He will go to the Bacchae , where he will pay the penalty with his death . Dionysus , now it is your job ; for you are not far off .
